name: Chronicles of Taldun: The Remainder
short_description: A dark fantasy tale where death is not the end but the path to the truth. Two magi trapped in a collapsing tower must unravel a deadly mystery. Each death reveals another piece of the puzzle that is their shared past and forbidden love. For fans of House in Fata Morgana and literary dark fantasy.
detailed_description:
Special Edition


About the Game

You wake in a collapsing tower with no memory. The only other person here—a silver-tongued mage named Ilar—claims to be saving your life through a desperate ritual. Yet, their story keeps changing and you keep dying.

This is Chronicles of Taldun: the Remainder, a dark fantasy visual novel where death is not the end—it's how you learn the truth.

Play as Vyn, a High Magus trapped with Ilar in a tower that's tearing itself apart. Each death reveals another fragment of memory, another piece of the mystery. Were you colleagues? Lovers? Enemies? The deeper you dig, the less certain you become, and the more you realize Ilar may not be trying to save you at all.

Read between the lines. Explore for clues. Use hands-on deduction to break the vicious circle and set them both free.

What Awaits You:

  • 172,000 words of bittersweet psychological horror (8-40 hours)

  • Over 500 choices, some more regrettable than others.

  • 50+ false endings and 3 true endings—dying teaches you how to live

  • Multiple romance paths with a nonbinary love interest (optional, fully avoidable)

  • Death-loop narrative structure where every playthrough reveals new layers

  • RPGFan Games of the Year 2022 - Editor's Choice Award

  • 96%+ Positive user reviews

"Bittersweet, hopeful, and utterly poignant... I was violently sobbing near the end." - Player Review

"A breathtaking narrative that explores memory, identity, and love across lifetimes." - Player Review

Also Featuring:

  • A magnificent cat who cares not if you live or die

  • An adorable eel friend

  • Death. Lots of it.

  • An angry potato

For fans of

The House in Fata Morgana, Disco Elysium, Umineko When They Cry, and literary dark fantasy by Susanna Clarke and Madeline Miller.
